Package org.eventb.core.pm

Interface IUserSupport

All Superinterfaces:
IElementChangedListener
All Known Implementing Classes:
UserSupport
public interface IUserSupport extends IElementChangedListener
Common protocol for manipulating the state of a proof and its proof tree.

A user support instance is associated with a Proof Status file, which is set with the setInput(IPSRoot) method. This method should be called only once, and prior to any other method call (except for dispose() which can be called at any time).

Once the input set, there is no proof obligation associated to a user support instance yet. A proof obligation will get associated when one of the following method is called:

  • setCurrentPO(IPSStatus, IProgressMonitor)
  • nextUndischargedPO(boolean, IProgressMonitor)
  • prevUndischargedPO(boolean, IProgressMonitor)
For the latter two, one needs in addition that there exists an undischarged proof obligation in the associated proof file.

This interface is not intended to be implemented by clients.

Since:
1.0

    Description

    elementChanged

  • Method Details

    • setInput

      void setInput(IPSRoot psRoot)
      Sets the input file root to associate with this user support.
      Parameters:
      psRoot - a proof status file root
      Since:
      3.0

    • dispose

      void dispose()
      Disconnects this user support from the Rodin Database and the user support manager.

    • getInput

      IPSRoot getInput()
      Returns the current input of the User Support which is a proof status file root.
      Returns:
      the input proof status file root of this User Support
      Since:
      3.0

    • loadProofStates

      void loadProofStates() throws RodinDBException
      Loads the proof statuses from the proof status file.
      Throws:
      RodinDBException

    • nextUndischargedPO

      void nextUndischargedPO(boolean force, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Go to the next undischarged proof obligation. If there is no undischarged obligation and the force flag is false then the User Support state is unchanged. If the force flag is true and there is no undischarged obligation then the current PO will be set to null.

      Parameters:
      force - a boolean flag
      monitor - a progress monitor
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - a Rodin Exception

    • prevUndischargedPO

      void prevUndischargedPO(boolean force, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Go to the previous undischarged proof obligation. If there is no undischarged obligation and the force flag is false then the User Support state is unchanged. If the force flag is true and there is no undischarged obligation then the current PO will be set to null.

      Parameters:
      force - a boolean flag
      monitor - a progress monitor
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - a Rodin Exception

    • getCurrentPO

      IProofState getCurrentPO()
      This method return the current Obligation (Proof State). This should be called at the initialization of a listener of the User Support. After that the listeners will update their states by listen to the changes from the User Support.

      Returns:
      the current ProofState (can be null).

    • setCurrentPO

      void setCurrentPO(IPSStatus psStatus, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Set the current PO corresponding to a specific proof obligation. The proof obligation is represented by a psStatus. If the psStatus is null then the current proof obligation is set to null. Otherwise, if there is no proof obligation found then the status of the User Support is unchanged.

      Parameters:
      psStatus - a psStatus within the file
      monitor - a progress monitor
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - a Rodin Exception

    • getPOs

      IProofState[] getPOs()
      Return all the proof obligations which are managed by this User Support.

      Returns:
      a collection of proof state stored in this User Support

    • hasUnsavedChanges

      boolean hasUnsavedChanges()
      Return true or false depending on if there are some unsaved proof obligations or not.

      Returns:
      true if there are some unsaved proof obligations stored in this User Support

    • getFormulaFactory

      @Deprecated FormulaFactory getFormulaFactory()
      Deprecated.
      use a formula factory from a proof
      Returns the formula factory to use for the current proof obligation.
      Returns:
      the formula factory to use according to the current PO
      Since:
      2.0

    • getUnsavedPOs

      IProofState[] getUnsavedPOs()
      Get an array of unsaved proof obligations.

      Returns:
      an array of unsaved proof obligations

    • removeCachedHypotheses

      void removeCachedHypotheses(Collection<Predicate> hyps)
      Remove a collection of hypotheses from the cache.

      Parameters:
      hyps - a collection of hypotheses

    • searchHyps

      void searchHyps(String token)
      Search the hypothesis set for a string token. In particular, if token is an empty string, all the hypotheses will be put in the set of searched hypotheses.

      Parameters:
      token - a string token

    • removeSearchedHypotheses

      void removeSearchedHypotheses(Collection<Predicate> hyps)
      Remove a collection of predicates from the searched hypothesis set.

      Parameters:
      hyps - a collection of predicates

    • selectNode

      void selectNode(IProofTreeNode pt)
      Select a node in the current obligation's proof tree.

      Parameters:
      pt - a proof tree node

    • applyTacticToHypotheses

      void applyTacticToHypotheses(ITactic t, Set<Predicate> hyps, boolean applyPostTactic, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor)
      Apply a tactic to the current proof obligation to a set of hypothesis

      Parameters:
      t - a proof tactic
      hyps - a set of hypothesis
      applyPostTactic - indicating if post tactic should be applied or not
      monitor - a progress monitor

    • applyTacticToHypotheses

      void applyTacticToHypotheses(ITactic t, Set<Predicate> hyps, ITacticRunner postTacticRunner, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor)
      Apply a tactic to the current proof obligation to a set of hypothesis

      The provided post tactic runner should execute its argument to run the post tactic; it can be done directly (e.g. runnable -> runnable.accept(newMonitor)) or through a worker thread, as long as the runner waits for the worker to finish before returning. If running the post tactic is not desired, please provide null rather than a runner that does nothing.

      Parameters:
      t - a proof tactic
      hyps - a set of hypothesis
      postTacticRunner - runner for the post tactic (or null if it should not be applied)
      monitor - a progress monitor
      Since:
      3.7

    • applyTactic

      void applyTactic(ITactic t, boolean applyPostTactic, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor)
      Apply a tactic to the current proof obligation at the current proof tree node.

      Parameters:
      t - a proof tactic
      applyPostTactic - indicating if post tactic should be applied or not
      monitor - a progress monitor

    • applyTactic

      void applyTactic(ITactic t, ITacticRunner postTacticRunner, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor)
      Apply a tactic to the current proof obligation at the current proof tree node.

      The provided post tactic runner should execute its argument to run the post tactic; it can be done directly (e.g. runnable -> runnable.accept(newMonitor)) or through a worker thread, as long as the runner waits for the worker to finish before returning. If running the post tactic is not desired, please provide null rather than a runner that does nothing.

      Parameters:
      t - a proof tactic
      postTacticRunner - runner for the post tactic (or null if it should not be applied)
      monitor - a progress monitor
      Since:
      3.7

    • back

      void back(org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor)
      Backtracking from the current proof tree node in the current proof obligation.

      Parameters:
      monitor - a progress monitor

    • setComment

      void setComment(String text, IProofTreeNode node)
      Set a comment for at a proof tree node.

      Parameters:
      text - a String (comment)
      node - a proof tree node

    • doSave

      void doSave(IProofState[] states, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Save the set of input proof obligations to disk.

      Parameters:
      states - an array of proof states to be saved
      monitor - a Progress Monitor
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if some error occurred while saving

    • selectNextSubgoal

      boolean selectNextSubgoal(boolean rootIncluded, IProofTreeNodeFilter filter)
      For the current proof state, select the next subgoal satisfies the filter. The flag rootIncluded to indicate that if the current node should be considered or not. If there is no such subgoal, do nothing.

      Parameters:
      rootIncluded - to include the current node or not
      filter - a proof tree node filter
      Returns:
      true if there is such a node satisfies the filter. Return false otherwise.
